headlines.headline: Sources: Suns, Jordan Goodwin set for 3-year, $19M deal
headlines.description: The Suns have reached a second deal this weekend, as free agent guard Jordan Goodwin intends to join Collin Gillespie in re-signing in Phoenix, sources told ESPN.

headlines.headline: Sources: Gillespie returning to Suns on 4-year, $48 million deal
headlines.description: Collin Gillespie, who is coming off a breakout campaign with the Suns, intends to sign a four-year, $48 million deal to remain with the team, sources told ESPN.

headlines.headline: Will the Patriots keep seven wide receivers on their roster?
headlines.description: Former Patriot Troy Brown evaluates the Patriots' most crowded position going into training camp.

headlines.description: Tennessee's newest coach is making his father, Sam, proud.

headlines.headline: Does Brendan Sorsby and the supplemental draft affect the Jets?
headlines.description: New York traded for Geno Smith this offseason, but could Sorsby be a possible solution long term?

headlines.headline: Philadelphia Eagles' Uar Bernard says Nigeria has NFL talent in abundance
headlines.description: Philadelphia Eagles rookie Uar Bernard believes Nigeria is filled with athletes capable of reaching the NFL, and wants the league to do more to create pathways for these young players to be discovered and developed.

headlines.headline: Could box breaking be considered gambling or a lottery?
headlines.description: A long-standing practice in the collectibles industry is under the microscope.

headlines.headline: Oilers re-sign Jason Dickinson to 5-year, $20M contract
headlines.description: The Edmonton Oilers re-signed Jason Dickinson to a five-year contract worth $20 million on Sunday, preventing the defensive-minded center from testing the free agent market.

headlines.headline: Hurricanes fans jam downtown Raleigh for Stanley Cup parade
headlines.description: Thousands of Hurricanes fans crammed onto sidewalks, gathered around office building windows, even lined up on floor after floor of at least one parking deck to cheer and wave at the team's Stanley Cup championship parade Saturday.

headlines.headline: Jonathan Toews, 3-time Stanley Cup champion, announces retirement
headlines.description: Jonathan Toews, a three-time Stanley Cup champion with the Blackhawks and two-time Olympic gold medalist with Canada, has announced his retirement.
